name: August Vilella
bio:
August Vilella is an artist from Barcelona based in Tokyo. His artworks have been exhibited around the world in cities such as Los Angeles, Hong Kong, Paris, Shanghai, Dubai, Tokyo, Amsterdam, Barcelona, Taipei, Oxford, Shenzhen, San Francisco, Rotterdam... etc. In 2016 he was the winner for The Best Artist at the Tokyo International Art Fair and in 2017 winner for the Best Innovative Art at The Global Art Awards of Dubai (UAE). In 2018 he won the "International Artist Grand Prize" of Taipei and the "Chairman's Award" in the Art Revolution Taipei where his work has been chosen as the best among 4,546 candidates.

He creates oil paintings by means of a surreal-intuitive method. Thanks to this process, he tries to give shape to his subconscious mind without using any previous sketches or ideas. The result of this practice evokes a dreamlike aura and a magical, metaphoric, and even philosophical language, which invites the observing visitor to reflect.

name: Arol
bio:
Born in Mar del Plata, Buenos Aires, Argentina. Between pencils and Legos, it was clear to him that creativity was as necessary for him as breathing. In 2003, in search of new challenges, he traveled to Spain where he has lived ever since. Although art has always been one of his great loves since he was a child, he had the opportunity to focus more on artistic works such as illustration. Despite having flirted with digital art since the 90's, it was not until 2010 that he decided to develop as an artist using pop surrealism as a language.

Through the light play of light and shadow, focus and backlight, in an almost theatrical staging, the whole of his work sinks its roots in a surreal, dreamlike, symbolic language that launches without filters and without reason the images rescued from the recesses of the unconscious. Figures that are both tender and disturbing, sometimes devoid of surroundings and represented in their own existence, rich in nuances and details.

name: Adam Handler
bio:
Childlike simplicity, quirky, colorful, primitive — these are just a few words to describe the extraordinary talent of New York-born painter Adam Handler. His recognizable characters such as bats, ghosts, and wide-eyed girls embody innocence and adolescent energy that the very best of artists will tell you is close to impossible to render with fresh authenticity.

Using acrylic paint, oil stick, pencil, and markers, Handler's child-like and passionate drawing technique produces distinctive outlined characters which almost appear flat, with little to no depth of field. Handler studied Life Drawing in Italy and later graduated from Purchase College with a degree in Art History. Handler's work has been exhibited internationally, namely throughout the United States, Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Middle East.

name: Cesc Abad
bio:
Born in Barcelona in 1973. At age of 15 he was expelled from school and it is during this time that he started being attracted to visual arts, especially painting. Everything was going well until his father suddenly died when he was 21 years old and he found himself at the head of his father's business.

This deeply influenced his way of understanding art, the world and even himself. After living a double life for the most part of twenty years, he decided to sell his companies in 2016 and dedicate himself exclusively to creating pieces of art. He decided his art was not a secret anymore...

name: Debbie Reda
bio:
Debbie Reda was born in Buenos Aires in 1976. Studied fashion design at the University of Buenos Aires (UBA) from 1993 to 1997. She has worked in the fashion production industry for 15 years. She holds a degree in Marketing Technology from the University of Palermo (UP) and graduated from ESEADE in 2017 with a degree in Curating and Cultural Management. She is also a professional classical ballet dancer and is currently expanding her exploration of movement through contemporary training and performance.

"Time and movement are two elements that are important for me to develop in the concept of my work. Every pose is altered through cycles and repetitions. It gives my work a certain weight over time." With an idea of nostalgic and mechanical structure, she adopted the forms of dots, crosses, ovals, circles and squares as primitive elements.

name: Esther Ziher
bio:
Born in the Netherlands with a pencil and brush in her hand and ever since she was a child an artlover in many ways. Esther's painting refers to small things in life which for her are the very most important. Such as love, family and friendship. Happiness, humour and warmth are the elements of her paintings.

She paints with acrylic on canvas and uses ink, ink spray, charcoal and oil pastels. She loves painting animals in a comical way and unrealistic as they have a very special place in her heart. The eyes of her characters have big round eyes and look a bit like goggle-eyes. The main characters are called Wipi (bird) and Pepe (frog). Nowadays Esther's paintings travel around the world to galleries, artdealers, art collectors and customers.

name: @Gonhdo
bio:
The creator of all the works made under the pseudonym @GONHDO is Gonzalo Hernández Sánchez de Ocaña, an artist born in Madrid in 1988. He has obtained a degree in Technical Architecture from the Polytechnic University of Madrid.

His training has been one of the keys to the development of his work, in addition to Madrid he has studied at New York University and at the National Autonomous University of Mexico. During his time in New York his admiration and attraction for two of the most prominent artistic movements of the 20th century was born, POP Art and Street Culture, represented by Keith Haring. His obsession with architecture, history, and the colour of the places he has visited bestow his art works with a great mastery of space and a special sensitivity that gives rise to expressive, joyful and contemporary pieces.

name: Jaime Sancorlo
bio:
Jaime Sancorlo (Madrid, 1980) was very young when he was already copying characters from films, comics and cartoons. Graduated in Fine Arts at the Complutense University of Madrid and trained at the Academy of Fine Arts in Warsaw with an Erasmus scholarship, it was in mid-2018, after attending a painting workshop given by Alejandro Carpintero, that he fell in love again with the practice of oil painting.

For him, Art could be any human creation capable of moving him and emanating what he considers to be beauty. Meticulous in his style, the technique with which this artist from Madrid feels most comfortable is definitely oil painting. He likes it for its long drying time, for its versatility, for its ability to melt or make sharp edges.

name: Keigo Nakamura
bio:
Keigo Nakamura is an artist born with a minimalist lifestyle, featuring small-scale works. With mature oil painting techniques influenced by 17th century French Baroque, he is pursuing values that are being lost in a fast modern society.

The composition of the picture centered on the light and shadow of the candle is inspired by the candlelight religious paintings painted by the 17th-century French painter "George de la Tour". The technique of arranging toys for indirect expression was inspired by "Sandplay Therapy" devised by the 20th century Swiss Jungian psychologist "Dora Calf". It is used as a means to symbolically express the inner world and complex concepts that are difficult to express in words.

name: Skolyshev
bio:
Five years of non-stop artistic searches. As a result, the artist created his own unique style. His character RED GIRL makes a strong impression. Always different in details and with a twist. Usually on a red background. The artist uses canvas and acrylic. Handmade in the best artistic traditions.

The artist is based in Belarus (Eastern Europe), from where he sends his canvases to the world.

name: Sniqus
bio:
He lives and works in Sabadell (Barcelona, Spain). Inspired by the words of one of the best Catalan surrealists Modest Cuixart about his quality of imagination and infinite creation of new forms he decides to start being part of the art world in 2003.

Since then he has learned several techniques such as oil, acrylic, airbrush, paint with inks, sprays that he later combines in one with a very marked style. His points, stripes, circles, hidden labyrinths come together to show you feelings, scenes of life and beyond, passions, failures and joys, motivational characters. Forms that demonstrate the virtues and defects of this magical-tragic experience called life.

name: Tocinor
bio:
"Hello! Look at my face" (Hola, mira mi cara) is one of Tocinor's most common phrases in the public media. It's also one of his few interpretations of his art. But there is no denying that when we are confronted with Tocinor's works, everything is self-evident — just look at our faces, no need for words, what you see and feel is the answer.

This self-taught Spanish artist, formerly known as David Sanchez Martin, is active as Tocinor in the field of artistic creation. His artistic universe grows out of a series of irregular combinations of shapes, all of which are squeezed to the point of flattening the moment they enter it. The shapes take on bright colors as the spatial cues are discarded. Perhaps Tocinor is revealing to us the track of joy in the code of light-hearted images: we don't have to be in a hurry to define what we see, we just need to trust our intuition.

name: Xavi Carbonell
bio:
Xavi Carbonell was born in Alcoy in 1971. His passion for art and a compelling need to express their feelings plastic, led him to enter the world of painting for over two decades. His main influences have not only come from the hands of artists like Basquiat, Cy Twombly, Saura and other Abstract Expressionism, but also of the doodles sketched by children.

His work has been exhibited in galleries, museums and art centers. He has been selected in numerous awards: XIII Painting Prize 2012 University of Murcia, the Third Biennial of Engraving Etching 2011 Valladolid, and in three editions of the Bancaja Prizes for Painting, Sculpture and Digital Art. His work is included in numerous public and private collections.
